sections.paragraph:
constr. צֵלַע and צֶלַע (of a segolate form), with suff. צַלְעִי f. (but pl. צְלָעִים m. 1Ki 6:34).

(1) a rib, Gen 2:21, 22 . Pl. צְלָעֹות beams (as if the ribs of a building, Gerippe, Rippenwerk), 1Ki 6:15, 16 1Ki 6:16 7:3 (compare verse 2, where there is כְּרֻתֹות in the same signification).

(2) a side-(a) of man, Job 18:12 Jer 20:10, שֹׁמְרֵי צַלְעִי “those who watch my side,” my friends who do not depart from my side.-(b) of inanimate things, as of the tabernacle, Exo 26:26, 27 Exo 26:27of the altar, Exo 27:7 of a quarter of the heaven, Exo 26:35. Pl. צְלָעִי (m.) sides, or leaves of a door, 1Ki 6:34. Elsewhere always צְלָעֹות, constr. צַלְעֹות the sides of the altar, Exo 38:7 of the ark of the covenant, Exo 25:14.

(3) a side chamber of the temple (as to which see יָצוּעַ ), 1Ki 6:5 Eze 41:6, seq.; also collect. story, or range of side chambers, 1Ki 6:8, and i.q. יָצוּעַ, used of the whole of that part of the temple, comprising three stories, Eze 41:5, Eze 41:9, 11 Eze 41:11. בֵּית צְלָעֹות Eze 41:9, is a space between the two walls of the temple, intended for these chambers. Compare Jos. Archæol. viii. 3, § 2.

(4) [Zelah], pr.n. of a town of the Benjamites, where Saul was buried, Jos 18:28 2Sa 21:14.
